description: Machine-readable design reference for the REASON design-critic (tempdoc 615 §11 REASON / §6.4 Judge). The 'foundation' rules are ALWAYS-ON (typography/spacing/z/motion scales — projected from the token system shell-v0/themes/token-names.generated.ts); the per-surface 'expect' blocks are the structural invariants a surface must satisfy; the 'rubric' lists the dimensions the LLM critic scores the measured facts against. Indeed's finding (§11): JSON wins as the design-system encoding — explicit keys, no ambiguity. Minimal-viable: one rubric, foundation + the key surfaces; expand as future work.

foundation.note: Spacing/sizing/z/motion MUST come from these token scales — a raw literal off-scale is a finding.

shell.note: Every surface renders inside the one shell: a banner, a navigation rail, and a single main landmark.

search.note: The default landing surface; the searchbox is the primary control.

rubric.ask: Are all required landmark roles present exactly once where unique (banner/navigation/main, single h1)?
Do headings descend without skipping a level (no h1 -> h3)?
Does the geometry imply a clear primary region and reading order, or do elements compete/overlap?
Do element positions/sizes sit on the 4px grid, or is there off-scale jitter?
Is the document free of unintended horizontal/vertical overflow?
Are there axe violations beyond the accepted baseline?
